factors.summary: The company is facing significant negative economic conditions primarily due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which has led to reduced consumer spending, lending activities, and disruptions in global capital markets. Additionally, the economic impact of COVID-19 has made it challenging to predict future performance against historic seasonality trends.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic and the mitigation efforts by governments to attempt to control its spread have adversely impacted the global economy, leading to reduced consumer spending and lending activities and disruptions and volatility in the global capital markets.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

evidence.text: Due to the COVID-19 pandemic, as described above within “Recent Events and Company Outlook,” we are unsure of how future results will compare to historic seasonality trends.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant negative economic impacts from COVID-19, leading to reduced spending and market disruptions.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed organic revenue growth trends, with evidence of both acceleration and deceleration across different business segments. While Verification Services and Employer Services are showing strong growth, other areas like non-mortgage related revenue in USIS and International have seen significant declines due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth is decelerating.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

evidence.text: We experienced a significant decline in revenue during the second half of March 2020. We expect that the negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on our operating revenue may continue until economic conditions improve.

Revenue increased by 48% in the first quarter of 2020, compared to the same period in 2019 due to strong growth in the mortgage and government verticals and continued addition of new records to The Work Number database. Workforce Solutions revenue increased by 32% in the first quarter of 2020, compared to the same period in 2019.
Revenue growth was negatively impacted due to a significant decline in the second half of March in non-mortgage related revenue in USIS and across International, due to the impact of COVID-19.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed growth trends with some segments declining due to COVID-19.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with significant increases in specific verticals such as mortgage and government, while overall demand has shown a deceleration due to the impact of COVID-19, particularly in other segments like commercial non-mortgage.

evidence.text: Overall demand is decelerating.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

evidence.text: We experienced a significant decline in revenue during the second half of March 2020. We expect that the negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on our operating revenue may continue until economic conditions improve.
Demand for Verification Services increased significantly, particularly in the mortgage and government verticals, while other segments faced declines due to COVID-19.

Revenue increased by 48% in the first quarter of 2020, compared to the same period in 2019 due to strong growth in the mortgage and government verticals and continued addition of new records to The Work Number database. In the second half of March, revenue from the commercial non-mortgage segment of Verification Services... began to see declines in revenue versus the same period in 2019 due to the impact of COVID-19.

Through the end of February 2020, consolidated revenue showed significant growth versus the same two-month period in 2019. Principally beginning in the second half of March, however, year-over-year consolidated revenue trends materially weakened.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating in some areas despite growth in specific verticals.

evidence.text: Further deteriorations in economic conditions, as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic or otherwise, could lead to a further or prolonged decline in demand for our products and services and negatively impact our business.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

evidence.text: Operating margin increased to 44.3% for the first quarter of 2020 from 42.1% for the first quarter of 2019. The increased margin was due to the increase in revenue and partially offset by increases in royalty, people, and technology costs.

factors.summary: The company faces key sensitivities related to economic conditions, particularly the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, and foreign exchange fluctuations in Latin America and Asia Pacific. These factors have negatively impacted revenue, with foreign exchange rates decreasing revenue by 2% and local currency fluctuations negatively impacting revenue by 7% and 8% in specific quarters.

evidence.text: The extent to which the coronavirus will continue to negatively impact our operations will depend on future developments which are highly uncertain and cannot be predicted with confidence, including the duration of the outbreak, new information which may emerge concerning the severity of the COVID-19 pandemic, outbreaks occurring at any of our facilities, the actions taken to control the spread of COVID-19 or treat its impact, and changes in worldwide and U.S. economic conditions.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

evidence.text: Local currency fluctuations against the U.S. dollar negatively impacted revenue by $15.8 million, or 7%, during the first quarter of 2020. Local currency fluctuations against the U.S. dollar negatively impacted revenue by $5.7 million, or 8%, for the first quarter of 2020. Local currency fluctuations had minimal impact on the first quarter of 2020.
Total revenue was negatively impacted by foreign exchange rates, which decreased revenue by $16.0 million, or 2%, for the first three months of 2020, compared to the same period in 2019.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions and foreign exchange fluctuations significantly negatively impacted revenue.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum is mixed, with growth in mortgage and government verticals offset by declines in commercial non-mortgage segments due to COVID-19.

evidence.text: Revenue increased by 48% in the first quarter of 2020, compared to the same period in 2019 due to strong growth in the mortgage and government verticals and continued addition of new records to The Work Number database.

In the second half of March, revenue from the commercial non-mortgage segment of Verification Services... began to see declines in revenue versus the same period in 2019 due to the impact of COVID-19.

The exception, particularly in the United States, is primarily in the mortgage market and our unemployment cost management business in which we are seeing significant growth, and in certain government services and our consumer direct businesses which are seeing far smaller impacts.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed growth across different market segments with some positive and some negative trends.

factors.summary: The company faces significant near-term risks, primarily from the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic, which is expected to continue affecting operating revenue and overall business performance. Additionally, there are risks related to pending legal payments from the 2017 cybersecurity incident, which could strain cash resources and require careful management of working capital. The company may also face challenges in cost management and potential asset impairment charges as a result of the pandemic.

evidence.text: We expect that the negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on our operating revenue may continue until economic conditions improve.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak .

evidence.text: As a result of the possible payments that could be made in 2020 related to the losses associated with certain legal proceedings and government investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident, funds generated by operating activities are not expected to be sufficient to fund working capital and other cash requirements throughout 2020.

We expect that the global COVID-19 pandemic will continue to adversely impact our business and results of operations. [...] Any future asset impairment charges, increase in allowance for doubtful accounts, or restructuring charges could be more likely and will be dependent on the severity and duration of this crisis.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant risks from COVID-19 pandemic and legal payments affecting cash resources and performance.

factors.summary: The company has implemented restructuring actions and cost reduction measures, which have positively impacted margins and reduced expenses. These actions include limiting new hires, reducing travel, and eliminating discretionary spending.

evidence.text: Restructuring actions have successfully reduced costs, particularly in the international segments, contributing to margin improvements.
The increased margin in the first quarter was due to a decrease in people costs driven by restructuring actions taken in 2019.

We have significantly limited the addition of new employees and third party contracted services, eliminated all travel except where necessary to meet customer or regulatory needs, and acted to limit discretionary spending.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction measures improved margins through restructuring and expense management.

factors.summary: The company has implemented headcount restructuring across its international segments and broader operations to reduce costs and improve efficiency. This restructuring includes limiting new hires, reducing travel, and eliminating non-essential expenses.

evidence.text: The increased margin in the first quarter was due to a decrease in people costs driven by restructuring actions taken in 2019.

We have significantly limited the addition of new employees and third party contracted services, eliminated all travel except where necessary to meet customer or regulatory needs, and acted to limit discretionary spending.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring actions reduced costs and improved efficiency by limiting hires and non-essential expenses.

factors.summary: Management exhibits a cautiously optimistic stance, supported by strong financial health and strategic investments in technology and security, yet acknowledges risks from legal proceedings and the lingering effects of COVID-19.

evidence.text: Management assesses liquidity in terms of our ability to generate cash to fund operating, investing and financing activities. We continue to generate substantial cash from operating activities, remain in a strong financial position, and manage our capital structure to meet short- and long-term objectives including reinvestment in existing businesses and strategic acquisitions.
Our critical priorities are [...] executing on our EFX2020 cloud technology, data and security transformation per our previously stated plans.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautiously optimistic with strong financial health and strategic investments.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditures have decreased in the first three months of 2020 compared to 2019, indicating a cautious approach to growth investments. However, they are expected to maintain similar levels of capital expenditures in 2020 as in 2019, focusing on technology transformation and enhancing technology systems, infrastructure, and data security.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures paid in the first three months of 2020 decreased by $26.8 million from the same period in 2019.
We are continuing to invest in enhanced technology systems, infrastructure and data security after the 2017 cybersecurity incident.
We expect to continue to incur significant expenses and capital expenditures in 2020 related to these initiatives, at similar levels as those incurred in 2019.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capex decreased but expected to remain similar, focusing on necessary investments.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage has increased due to borrowings and the issuance of senior notes, but it has taken steps to enhance financial flexibility by amending its credit facility and maintaining strong liquidity.

evidence.text: There were $125.0 million of borrowings under the Receivables Facility as of March 31, 2020. The Receivables Facility was supported by $276.1 million of accounts receivable as collateral at March 31, 2020 which, as a retained interest, is included in accounts receivable, net in our Consolidated Balance Sheets.
We recently amended our revolving credit facility to increase the maximum leverage ratio through 2021 to provide us with additional financial flexibility.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt increased but steps were taken to enhance financial flexibility.

factors.summary: The company is actively engaging in mergers and acquisitions, particularly with the completion of the India joint venture acquisition in the first quarter of 2020, which indicates strategic expansion and resulted in a significant gain.

evidence.text: During the first three months of 2020, we acquired the remaining interest in our India joint venture, within the International segment.
We completed the acquisition of the remaining shareholder interests in the first quarter of 2020, resulting in a significant gain.
evidence.subsection: Interest Expense and Other Income, net

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active M&A activity indicating strategic expansion and gains.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation appears to be stable. Both subsections indicate that cash provided by operating activities was similar or relatively flat compared to the prior year period. However, there is a slight difference in the level of detail provided, with one mentioning offsets due to certain legal proceedings and the other simply stating similarity in cash provided by operating activities. Overall, the evidence suggests stability in free cash flow generation despite minor contextual differences in the reporting.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities in the three months ended March 31, 2020 was relatively flat compared the prior year period. The increased net income was offset by the partial payment of accrued losses associated with certain legal proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident, net of insurance proceeds, and decreases in net working capital.
Cash provided by operating activities was $30.8 million during the first three months of 2020, similar to the prior year period.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2.  MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable free cash flow despite minor contextual differences.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure remains significant due to the 2017 cybersecurity incident. There is a potential for additional losses, and the company has already incurred substantial payments related to legal settlements.

evidence.text: The Company has and expects to make payments to resolve certain legal proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident, described more fully in 'Item 1. Legal Proceedings' in this Form 10-Q. Through March 31, 2020, the Company made payments of $391.2 million for legal settlements related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident.

We believe it is probable that we will incur losses associated with certain of the proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident. [...] The ultimate amount paid on these actions, claims and investigations [...] could be material to the Company’s consolidated financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows in future periods.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant ongoing litigation with potential for additional losses.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns through dividends remain stable, with no changes in the dividend policy.

evidence.text: We maintained our quarterly dividend of $0.39 per share in the first quarter of 2019. We paid cash dividends to Equifax shareholders of $47.3 million and $47.1 million, or $0.39 per share, during the three months ended March 31, 2020 and 2019, respectively.

We did not repurchase shares of our common stock during the first three months of 2020. [...] We paid out $47.3 million or $0.39 per share in dividends to our shareholders during the first three months of 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Dividends stable with no changes in policy.
